role_adherence_notes.linguistic: Correctly identified lack of visible text; stayed within domain.
role_adherence_notes.landscape: Analyzed terrain (volcanic rock) and climate cues (tropical/subtropical) correctly.
role_adherence_notes.botanics: Analyzed vegetation types (broadleaf, palms) and biome consistency.
role_adherence_notes.regulatory: Analyzed road infrastructure (guardrails, markings) and driving side.
role_adherence_notes.meta: Synthesized cross-domain cues (camera generation, vehicle models) to support the US hypothesis.

verdict.overall_verdict_notes: The pipeline failed because the ground truth (France/Reunion) was not in the candidate pool. The agents correctly identified tropical/subtropical cues but defaulted to the USA due to strong regulatory/meta biases (W-beam guardrails, Google Street View artifacts) that are shared by Reunion. The tournament then correctly selected the USA from the pool, but the pool itself was flawed.

visual_consistency_notes.landscape: Accurately described volcanic rock and tropical vegetation.
visual_consistency_notes.botanics: Accurately described broadleaf trees and palms.
visual_consistency_notes.regulatory: Accurately described W-beam guardrails and road markings.
visual_consistency_notes.meta: Accurately described vehicle models and camera artifacts.

tournament_failure.failure_reasoning: The ground truth (France/Reunion) was not included in the candidate pool. The landscape, botanics, and regulatory agents all focused on the USA as the primary candidate due to shared visual features (tropical vegetation, W-beam guardrails, right-hand traffic) and failed to consider Reunion as a French territory with similar infrastructure.
